sections.paragraph:
πολυπλάσιος, α, ον (on the form s. Schwyzer I 446; 2 Macc 9:16; Philo; Agis, Anth. Pal. 6, 152; PPanop 2, 232 [III a.d.]) manifold καρπὸν δικαιοσύνης αὐτοῦ μονοούσιον πολυπλάσιον his harvest of uprightness, unique and diverse GJs 6:3 (codd.; s. deStrycker 225–27. The pap and other codd. have the otherwise unknown πολυπλούσιον ‘abundantly rich’).—DELG s.v. πέλας.

Schwyzer Schwyzer = ES., Griechische Grammatik—List 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 847
